title: Henckelia alba
intro: Henckelia alba, also known as the white Indian pipe cactus, is a plant species in the Gesneriaceae family, native to the tropical forests of India. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Henckelia alba include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Henckelia alba is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is also used to make herbal teas and infusions.
